hypothesis: A cohesive, read-only Google integration package with bounded transport, truthful evidence states, canonical seoctl wiring, and adversarial tests can meet or exceed the comparator's code-contract quality while leaving live parity explicitly open until authorized credentials exist.

strongest_objections: Live provider proof is unavailable, so every live-smoke parity row must remain GAP_OPEN and the live state must remain BLOCKED.

The first draft omitted required aggregate, current CrUX, URL Inspection canonical naming, and sitemap status commands; closure requires all nine commands in the canonical registry and generated documentation.
Search Console detail rows can omit anonymized data, so visible rows must never be represented as property totals and aggregate evidence must come from a separate dimensionless request.
A green fixture suite alone must not be described as live integration parity or production traffic validation.
Operator value would be overstated if missing credentials produced stack traces or false empty results.
residual_risks: Real account permission and quota responses remain unobserved without authorized credentials.
Google contract changes after the documented verification date require revalidation.

strongest_objections: OAuth refresh responses were initially not size-bounded; approval requires the token path to enforce the same timeout, host, redirect, JSON, and response-size controls as API traffic.
API keys, access tokens, refresh tokens, and encoded credential variants must never appear in generated URLs, logs, exceptions, or tracked files.
Optional Google capabilities must not introduce a persistent token store, a second retry subsystem, a second URL-safety implementation, or mandatory heavy dependencies.
Sitemap behavior must remain read-only by default, with no submission or deletion side effect hidden behind a status command.
Rollback must require only reverting the phase commit and removing environment credentials, with no schema or data migration.
residual_risks: Live OAuth refresh, provider quotas, and permission failures remain blocked by unavailable credentials.
DNS and outbound-network policy can differ across deployment environments and must be validated during authorized smoke tests.

lessons: Search Console property totals require a separate dimensionless request because dimension rows may omit anonymized or lower-volume data.
Missing provider records, missing permissions, rate limits, invalid responses, and missing credentials require distinct truthful states rather than zero or empty success.
OAuth token refresh must use the same bounded host, redirect, timeout, size, JSON, and redaction controls as API requests.
Current CrUX, CrUX History, and LCP subparts are separate evidence products and must not be blended.
Fixture-complete production-capable code may be approved while live-smoke parity remains GAP_OPEN and live_state remains BLOCKED.
next_action: Submit the green production-capable code contract for normal squash merge while preserving GAP_OPEN live-smoke rows; execute authorized read-only smoke tests when credentials become available.
